vol_description: Each video represents an experimental session in which infants were familiarized to stimuli for various durations, and then their looking to either the familiar or a new stimulus was measured.

vol_name: Early concepts of intimacy: young humans use saliva sharing to infer thick relationships
vol_description: This is a subset of participant videos from Studies 2.E and 3.E. They are trimmed so that only the stimuli are present to avoid revealing identifying information such as the child's name that make have been spoken by the experimenter during set up.

vol_name: Replications of classic studies from infant cognition
vol_description: This includes videos of infants who watched animated videos showing violations of psychological or physical expectations. All videos are of sessions in which parents/caregivers agreed to have the session recordings shared with researchers or the public. For the goal (GS) study, infants ranged from 4.77 to 9.99 months; for the support condition of the physics (PHYS) study, infants ranged from 4:54 to 10.36 months; and for the solidity condition of the physics (PHYS) study, infants ranged from 4.77 to 9.99 months.

-- Project Description -- 
"In this ongoing project we aim to investigate a fundamental feature of human social cognition: Theory of Mind (ToM), or the ability to ascribe mental states to agents. One of the central tests for ToM is the false belief task, in which the participant needs to predict how an agent will act on the basis of her/his (mis)representation of the world. Using a false belief task (3D-animated videos), we ask if infants (18- to 27-month-olds) and adults show anticipatory looking that reflects the false belief of another agent." Source: https://manybabies.org/MB2/. 

Saxelab collected data as part of the Many Babies 2 project, and this repository contains only the data from this lab. Emily Chen (echen15@mit.edu) is the lead investigator and point of contact for all contributions from Saxelab for this project. 

Data were collected between August and October 2023. N = 5 pilot subjects and N = 31 non-pilot subjects consented to participate. 

-- Consent to Share -- 
This folder contains all of the raw data for participants who consented to share data via Databrary. Participants were able to choose from four levels of privacy, with "Level 0" being withdrawal of data from the study, "Level 1" being keeping data private to only Saxelab researchers (and thus not shared on Databrary), "Level 2" allowing data to be shared on Databrary for further researcher and educational purposes, and "Level 3" allowing data to be shared on Databrary and used in more public forums like social media to spread the word about other experiments in the lab. See here (https://drive.google.com/file/d/1pVQhkdq5IdWXblOYocq8DZp0uMx8b_tG/view) for the consent form with more detail about data sharing. 

-- Experimental Paradigm -- 
Each participant saw 6 trials: 4 familiarization trials and 2 test trials. Each trial is an individual video file. Note that participants saw a random ordering of the familiarization trials and a random ordering of the test trials. 

Each trial has two different versions of a video, so there are a total of 12 videos in each subject’s folder (6 trials x 2 video versions = 12 videos). One version has filenames that start with “audio” and the other has filenames that start with “tagged.” The “tagged” videos have a blue overlay dot that is meant to show in real time where the Webgazer eye-tracking tool decided the subject was looking on the screen. The “audio” videos don’t have the blue dot overlay but contains the recording audio of what was happening during the session. The “tagged” video is the one used for the main quality control checks to make sure that the blue overlay dot lines up with the gaze direction of the subject (see below for exact exclusion criteria). The “audio” video for each trial allows for quality control on interference from external sources (e.g., a parent or background discussion).
